description: The `is .Variant` operator applied to a non-enum-typed left operand fires E-TYPE-062 (§18.17). `name` is a string local; `name is .Admin` compares a string against an enum variant, which is ill-typed, so the type system reports E-TYPE-062 (the `is` operator requires an enum-typed LHS). Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §18.17 the `is .Variant` discrimination operator requires an enum-typed left-hand operand; applying it to a string/number/other non-enum value is a compile error (E-TYPE-062).
